title: ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED for WebGL Brotli-compressed .data.br when patched with USSR v1.1.8
author: scozirge
body:
**Version**  
- USSR-v1.1.8-net6.0-win-x64.zip
- Unity 2022.3.46f1  

**Environment**  
- Browser: Chrome (latest)  
- Hosting: Google Cloud Storage (using gsutil setmeta to set Content-Encoding: br and correct Content-Type headers)  

**Steps to reproduce**  
1. In Unity, enable **Publishing Settings → Compression Format → Brotli** and build a WebGL project.  
2. Locate the generated Build_WebGL.data.br.  
3. Run USSR, choose **Remove Unity Splash Screen**, and select that .data.br file.  
4. USSR creates a backup and patches the .data.br in place.  
5. Upload the entire Build/ folder to GCS
6. Open index.html in Chrome.  

**Actual result**  
- Chrome fails to load the patched file and reports:

title: How to remove the background image fade?
author: inweaveapp
body:
Thank you for building this tool! It has worked great for the purposes advertised, and functions cleanly for Windows, Mac, and Linux Standalone.

Using Unity 2022.3.24f1 targeting Windows and Linux, I notice that it does away with the Unity logo but it leaves the fade that is put over the background image. For example, if you choose "Dark on Light" in the Player Settings for their logo, there is about a 25% darkening effect applied over the background image. If you choose "Light on Dark" it does this in reverse. It isn't a huge deal but it does modify the background image to look different than intended, which is slightly noticeable even without the logo.

Is it possible for the tool to remove this as well? I notice on the Mac standalone it simply does away with the splash screen entirely, replacing it with black for a few seconds. This could just be idiosyncrasies of Unity and this version of the engine, but having the behavior be consistent or atleast removing the fade placed over the background image would be a plus.

When you have a moment, please let me know if this is possible. Thank you for your time!

title: WebGL corruption leading to Maximum call stack size exceeded
author: activesand
body:
Been using this tool without any problem on all platforms, including WebGL (without compression), however as I'm looking to improve performances on WebGL I tried again with brotli (and gzip) compression, but when using this tool it leads to the error "Unhandled promise rejection: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded" when starting the app.
The app works when I don't use USSR.

Version: I tried from 1.1.6 to 1.1.8
Unity version: 2022.3.10f (tried some others too)

What can be the cause of the problem?

title: [UPDATE] USSR will have a GUI for future updates
author: kiraio-moe
body:
Controlling the flow of application with `label` and `goto` is a bad idea. It's make me hard to debug the code.
For the future update, I'll use Avalonia as the front-end of choice cause it's cross-platform. The implementation will use MVVM pattern.

Stay tuned for the next update, it's my first time creating a desktop app so it will take some time.
Any thoughts? Don't hesitate to comment.

title: [REQUEST] Support Decompression Fallback in WebGL
author: ULE7
body:
So I turn on the splash logo at 2020.1.0 and made a webgl package, the USSR looked worked fine, However when I tried to open this webgl program it got stuck somehow, and never entered inside program. By the way I am using 1.1.5 USSR, already choose "All Sequential". 
I also tried 2022.3.0 unity it turns the same. I noticed I have marked "Decompression fallback" true in Player setting/Publishing setting which generate the .unityweb file instead of .gz files(gz is unusable for me). Seriously, I tried a lot times in different version of Unity, and never saw this USSR actually working even for once.........
